title: Dadar Impersonation Fraud
description: A 67-year-old retired government employee was defrauded of Rs 4.2 lakhs by a caller posing as a CBI officer. Victim was told his Aadhaar was linked to a money laundering case and instructed to transfer funds to a safe account for verification. Three calls over two days.
modus_operandi: Phone-based impersonation of law enforcement, fear and urgency tactics, digital transfer to mule accounts, elderly victim targeting.

suspects.description: Unknown, operated via spoofed number, Hindi-speaking with neutral accent

evidence.description: Spoofed call originated from VoIP number routed through a server in Rajasthan
Funds transferred to mule account in Jharkhand, withdrawn within 2 hours
VoIP server IP traced to a registered cyber cafe in Bharatpur

blind_spots: Mule account flagging delayed by 3 days — funds already withdrawn
VoIP trace deprioritised initially as out-of-jurisdiction
No cross-reference with Jharkhand cyber crime cell done until day 9
resolution: Cyber cafe CCTV identified operator. Coordinated arrest with Rajasthan police on day 16.
